2612.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever is so occupied with reciting the Quran that he has no time to ask of Me and remember Me, I shall give him the best reward of those who ask. And the superiority of the speech of Allah over all other speech is like the superiority of Allah over His creation."
2612.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang disibukkan oleh membaca Al-Qur'an sehingga tidak sempat memohon kepada-Ku dan berzikir kepada-Ku, maka Aku akan memberikan kepadanya pahala terbaik dari orang-orang yang memohon. Dan keutamaan kalam Allah atas seluruh kalam lainnya adalah seperti keutamaan Allah atas makhluk-Nya."

2613.iwh_en: It was narrated from Shahr ibn Hawshab in a mursal chain that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The superiority of the speech of Allah over the speech of His creation is like the superiority of Allah over His creation."
2613.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari Shahr ibn Hawshab bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Keutamaan kalam Allah atas kalam makhluk-Nya adalah seperti keutamaan Allah atas makhluk-Nya."

2614.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Quran is more beloved to Allah than the heavens and the earth and all that is in them."
2614.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Al-Qur'an lebih dicintai Allah daripada langit, bumi, dan seluruh yang ada di dalamnya."

2615.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jundub (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Recite the Quran as long as your hearts are in harmony over it. When you disagree about it, then rise and leave."
2615.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jundub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Bacalah Al-Qur'an selama hati-hati kalian bersatu padanya. Apabila kalian berselisih tentangnya, maka bangkit dan pergilah."

2616.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jundub ibn 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Recite the Quran as long as your hearts are in harmony over it. When you disagree about it, then rise and leave."
2616.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jundub ibn 'Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Bacalah Al-Qur'an selama hati-hati kalian bersatu padanya. Apabila kalian berselisih tentangnya, maka bangkit dan pergilah."

2617.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jundub (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Recite the Quran as long as your hearts are in harmony over it. When you disagree about it, then rise and leave."
2617.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jundub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Bacalah Al-Qur'an selama hati-hati kalian bersatu padanya. Apabila kalian berselisih tentangnya, maka bangkit dan pergilah."

2618.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Among people, there are those who are given faith but not the Quran, and among them are those who are given the Quran but not faith, and among them are those who are given both the Quran and faith, and among them are those who are given neither the Quran nor faith." Then he gave a parable for each: "As for the one given faith but not the Quran, he is like a date — sweet in taste but with no fragrance. As for the one given the Quran but not faith, he is like the myrtle — pleasant in fragrance but bitter in taste. As for the one given both the Quran and faith, he is like the citron — pleasant in fragrance and sweet in taste. As for the one given neither the Quran nor faith, he is like the colocynth — bitter in taste and with no fragrance."
2618.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Di antara manusia ada yang diberi iman tetapi tidak diberi Al-Qur'an, ada yang diberi Al-Qur'an tetapi tidak diberi iman, ada yang diberi Al-Qur'an dan iman, dan ada yang tidak diberi Al-Qur'an maupun iman." Kemudian ia membuat perumpamaan: "Adapun yang diberi iman tanpa Al-Qur'an, perumpamaannya seperti kurma — manis rasanya tetapi tidak beraroma. Adapun yang diberi Al-Qur'an tanpa iman, perumpamaannya seperti pohon myrtle — wangi aromanya tetapi pahit rasanya. Adapun yang diberi Al-Qur'an dan iman, perumpamaannya seperti buah citron — wangi aromanya dan manis rasanya. Adapun yang tidak diberi Al-Qur'an maupun iman, perumpamaannya seperti buah hanzhalah — pahit rasanya dan tidak beraroma."

2619.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "The parable of the believer who recites the Quran is like the citron — its taste is good and its fragrance is good. The parable of the believer who does not recite the Quran is like the date — its taste is sweet but it has no fragrance. The parable of the hypocrite who recites the Quran is like the basil — its fragrance is good but its taste is bitter. The parable of the hypocrite who does not recite the Quran is like the colocynth — it has no fragrance and its taste is bitter."
2619.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Musa al-Asy'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Perumpamaan orang mukmin yang membaca Al-Qur'an adalah seperti buah citron — rasanya enak dan aromanya wangi. Perumpamaan orang mukmin yang tidak membaca Al-Qur'an adalah seperti kurma — rasanya manis tetapi tidak beraroma. Perumpamaan orang munafik yang membaca Al-Qur'an adalah seperti tanaman kemangi — aromanya wangi tetapi rasanya pahit. Perumpamaan orang munafik yang tidak membaca Al-Qur'an adalah seperti buah hanzhalah — tidak beraroma dan rasanya pahit."

2620.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The parable of the one given faith but not the Quran is like the date — its taste is good but it has no fragrance. The parable of the one given the Quran but not faith is like the fragrant myrtle — its fragrance is good but its taste is bitter. The parable of the one given both the Quran and faith is like the citron — its fragrance is good and its taste is good. The parable of the one given neither faith nor the Quran is like the colocynth — its odor is foul and its taste is foul."
2620.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Perumpamaan orang yang diberi iman tetapi tidak diberi Al-Qur'an adalah seperti kurma — rasanya enak tetapi tidak beraroma. Perumpamaan orang yang diberi Al-Qur'an tetapi tidak diberi iman adalah seperti tanaman myrtle yang wangi — aromanya wangi tetapi rasanya pahit. Perumpamaan orang yang diberi Al-Qur'an dan iman adalah seperti buah citron — aromanya wangi dan rasanya enak. Perumpamaan orang yang tidak diberi iman maupun Al-Qur'an adalah seperti buah hanzhalah — aromanya busuk dan rasanya busuk."

2621.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amir ibn Wathila that Nafi' ibn 'Abd al-Harith met 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) at 'Usfan — for 'Umar had appointed him over the people of Mecca. Nafi' greeted 'Umar, and 'Umar said to him: "Whom did you appoint as your deputy over the people of the valley?" Nafi' said: "I appointed Ibn Abza over them." 'Umar asked: "And who is Ibn Abza?" Nafi' replied: "A freed slave among our freed slaves." 'Umar said: "You appointed a freed slave as your deputy over them?" Nafi' answered: "O Commander of the Faithful, he is a reciter of the Book of Allah and knowledgeable in the laws of inheritance." 'Umar then said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah raises some peoples by means of this Book and lowers others by it.'"
2621.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amir ibn Wathilah bahwa Nafi' ibn 'Abd al-Harith berjumpa dengan 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) di 'Usfan — karena 'Umar telah mengangkatnya sebagai pemimpin atas penduduk Mekah. Nafi' mengucapkan salam kepada 'Umar, lalu 'Umar bertanya: "Siapa yang kamu angkat sebagai wakilmu atas penduduk lembah?" Nafi' menjawab: "Aku mengangkat Ibn Abza atas mereka." 'Umar bertanya: "Siapa Ibn Abza?" Nafi' menjawab: "Seorang budak yang telah dimerdekakan dari kalangan budak-budak kami." 'Umar berkata: "Kamu mengangkat seorang budak merdeka sebagai wakilmu atas mereka?" Nafi' menjawab: "Wahai Amirul Mukminin, ia adalah seorang yang hafal Kitabullah dan mengetahui ilmu faraid." 'Umar kemudian berkata: "Sesungguhnya Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah mengangkat derajat sebagian kaum dengan Kitab ini dan merendahkan yang lain dengannya.'"

2622.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn Ma'dan that he said: "The one who recites the Quran earns one reward, and the one who listens to it earns two rewards."
2622.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn Ma'dan bahwa ia berkata: "Orang yang membaca Al-Qur'an mendapat satu pahala, sedangkan orang yang mendengarkannya mendapat dua pahala."

2623.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever listens to even one verse from the Book of Allah, it shall be a light for him."
2623.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa mendengarkan satu ayat dari Kitabullah, maka itu akan menjadi cahaya baginya."

2624.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'A'isha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "The one who recites the Quran and is proficient in it shall be with the noble and righteous scribes (the angels). And the one who recites it while it is difficult for him shall have two rewards."
2624.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Orang yang membaca Al-Qur'an dan mahir di dalamnya akan bersama para malaikat yang mulia dan berbakti. Sedangkan orang yang membacanya dan merasa kesulitan, maka baginya dua pahala."

2625.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wahb al-Dhimari that he said: "Whoever is given the Quran by Allah and stands with it during the night and day, acts upon what is in it, and dies in a state of obedience — Allah shall resurrect him on the Day of Judgment alongside the scribes and those who judge. (Sa'id explained: 'The scribes are the angels, and those who judge are the prophets.') As for the one who is zealous but it keeps slipping from him, yet he does not abandon it — he shall receive his reward twice over. And whoever is zealous over it but it keeps slipping from him and he dies in a state of obedience — he is among the most noble of them, and they are preferred over other people just as eagles are preferred over other birds, and as a lush green meadow is preferred over the surrounding land. On the Day of Judgment it will be said: 'Where are those who used to recite My Book, who were not distracted by following their livestock?' And they shall be given everlasting life and bliss. If both of his parents died in a state of obedience, crowns of kingship shall be placed upon their heads, and they shall say: 'Our Lord, our deeds did not reach this.' And it will be said: 'Yes, for your son used to recite My Book.'"
2625.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wahb al-Dhimari bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa yang Allah anugerahkan Al-Qur'an kepadanya, lalu ia mendirikannya di malam dan siang hari, mengamalkan isinya, dan meninggal dalam ketaatan — Allah akan membangkitkannya pada hari kiamat bersama para malaikat penulis dan para hakim yang adil. (Sa'id menjelaskan: 'Para malaikat penulis adalah malaikat, dan para hakim adalah para nabi.') Adapun orang yang bersemangat namun Al-Qur'an terus lepas darinya, tetapi ia tidak meninggalkannya — ia akan mendapat pahalanya dua kali lipat. Dan barangsiapa yang bersemangat namun Al-Qur'an terus lepas darinya dan ia meninggal dalam ketaatan — ia termasuk yang paling mulia di antara mereka, dan mereka diutamakan atas orang lain sebagaimana elang diutamakan atas burung-burung lainnya, dan sebagaimana padang rumput hijau yang subur diutamakan atas tanah-tanah sekitarnya. Pada hari kiamat akan dikatakan: 'Mana orang-orang yang dahulu membaca Kitab-Ku, yang tidak lalai dengan mengikuti ternak mereka?' Lalu mereka diberi keabadian dan kenikmatan abadi. Jika kedua orang tuanya meninggal dalam ketaatan, mahkota kerajaan akan diletakkan di kepala mereka, lalu keduanya berkata: 'Wahai Tuhan kami, amalan kami tidak sampai pada ini.' Dan dikatakan: 'Benar, karena anakmu dahulu membaca Kitab-Ku.'"

2626.iwh_en: It was narrated in a mursal chain from 'Abd al-Malik ibn 'Umayr that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "In the Opening of the Book (al-Fatiha) there is a cure for every ailment."
2626.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari 'Abd al-Malik ibn 'Umayr bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Dalam pembuka Kitab (al-Fatihah) terdapat penyembuh bagi setiap penyakit."

2627.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id ibn al-Mu'alla al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by me and said: 'Did Allah not say: "O you who believe, respond to Allah and the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life, and know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart, and that to Him you shall be gathered" [Al-Anfal: 24]? Shall I not teach you the greatest surah in the Quran before I leave the mosque?' When he was about to leave, he said: 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabb al-'alamin [Praise be to Allah, Lord of all the worlds]' — it is the Seven Oft-Repeated Verses and the Mighty Quran that you have been given."
2627.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id ibn al-Mu'alla al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ melewatiku dan bersabda: 'Bukankah Allah berfirman: "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, penuhilah seruan Allah dan Rasul apabila ia menyeru kalian kepada sesuatu yang menghidupkan kalian, dan ketahuilah bahwa Allah membatasi antara seseorang dan hatinya, dan bahwa kepada-Nyalah kalian akan dikumpulkan" [Al-Anfal: 24]? Maukah aku ajarkan kepadamu surah yang paling agung dalam Al-Qur'an sebelum aku keluar dari masjid?' Ketika beliau hendak keluar, beliau bersabda: 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabb al-'alamin' — itulah as-Sab'ul Matsani dan Al-Qur'an al-'Azhim yang telah diberikan kepadamu."

2628.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Opening of the Book (Fatihat al-Kitab) is the Seven Oft-Repeated Verses."
2628.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubayy ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Pembuka Kitab (Fatihat al-Kitab) adalah as-Sab'ul Matsani."

2629.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Nothing like it has been revealed in the Torah, the Gospel, the Psalms, or the Quran" — meaning Umm al-Quran — "and it is the Seven Oft-Repeated Verses and the Mighty Quran that I have been given."
2629.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak ada yang semisal dengannya yang diturunkan dalam Taurat, Injil, Zabur, maupun Al-Qur'an" — yakni Umm al-Qur'an — "dan ia adalah as-Sab'ul Matsani dan Al-Qur'an al-'Azhim yang telah diberikan kepadaku."

2630.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Al-hamdu lillah (Praise be to Allah) is the Mother of the Quran, the Mother of the Book, and the Seven Oft-Repeated Verses."
2630.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Al-hamdulillah adalah Ummul Qur'an, Ummul Kitab, dan as-Sab'ul Matsani."

2631.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "There is no house in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited except that the devil leaves it while passing wind."
2631.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Tidak ada rumah yang dibacakan di dalamnya Surah al-Baqarah kecuali setan keluar darinya sambil kentut."

2632.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn Ma'dan that he said: "Learning Surah al-Baqarah is a blessing, abandoning it is a loss, and the sorcerers cannot withstand it — it is the pinnacle tent of the Quran."
2632.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn Ma'dan bahwa ia berkata: "Mempelajari Surah al-Baqarah adalah berkah, meninggalkannya adalah kerugian, dan para tukang sihir tidak mampu menandinginya — ia adalah tenda puncak Al-Qur'an."

2633.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Everything has a peak, and the peak of the Quran is Surah al-Baqarah. Everything has a core, and the core of the Quran is the Mufassal surahs." — Abu Muhammad said: "Al-lubab means the purest part."
2633.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Setiap sesuatu memiliki puncak, dan puncak Al-Qur'an adalah Surah al-Baqarah. Setiap sesuatu memiliki inti, dan inti Al-Qur'an adalah surah-surah al-Mufassal." — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Al-lubab artinya bagian yang paling murni."

2634.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad that he said: "Whoever recites Surah al-Baqarah, a crown shall be placed upon him in Paradise."
2634.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Surah al-Baqarah, ia akan dipakaikan mahkota di surga."

2635.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "When the devil hears Surah al-Baqarah being recited in a house, he leaves it."
2635.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Apabila setan mendengar Surah al-Baqarah dibacakan di suatu rumah, ia akan keluar darinya."

2636.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ayfa' ibn 'Abd al-Kala'i in a mursal chain that a man said: "O Messenger of Allah, which surah of the Quran is the greatest?" He ﷺ said: "Say: He is Allah, One (Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad)." The man asked: "And which verse in the Quran is the greatest?" He ﷺ said: "The Verse of the Throne: 'Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining' [Al-Baqarah: 255]." The man said: "Which verse, O Prophet of Allah, would you most love to be bestowed upon you and your ummah?" He ﷺ said: "The concluding verses of Surah al-Baqarah, for they are from the treasures of the mercy of Allah, from beneath His Throne — He gave them to this ummah. They encompass every good of this world and the Hereafter."
2636.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan secara mursal dari Ayfa' ibn 'Abd al-Kala'i bahwa seorang laki-laki berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, surah apa dalam Al-Qur'an yang paling agung?" Beliau ﷺ menjawab: "Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad." Laki-laki itu bertanya: "Dan ayat apa dalam Al-Qur'an yang paling agung?" Beliau ﷺ menjawab: "Ayat Kursi: 'Allah, tidak ada ilah selain Dia, Yang Maha Hidup lagi Maha Berdiri Sendiri' [Al-Baqarah: 255]." Laki-laki itu bertanya: "Ayat apa, wahai Nabi Allah, yang paling engkau sukai untuk diberikan kepadamu dan umatmu?" Beliau ﷺ menjawab: "Penutup Surah al-Baqarah, karena ia berasal dari perbendaharaan rahmat Allah dari bawah 'Arsy-Nya — Allah memberikannya kepada umat ini. Ia mencakup setiap kebaikan dunia dan akhirat."

2637.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "A man from the Companions of Muhammad wrestled a jinn and threw the jinn down. The man said to the jinn: 'I see you are small and thin — your forearms are like a dog's forearms. Is that how you jinn all are, or is it only you?' The jinn said: 'No, by Allah, I am among the stout ones among them. But wrestle me again, and if you throw me down I will teach you something that will benefit you.' The man wrestled him again and threw him down. He said: 'Give it to me — teach me.' The jinn said: 'Yes. Do you recite the Verse of the Throne: "Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining" [Al-Baqarah: 255]?' He replied: 'Yes.' The jinn said: 'If you recite it in a house, the devil leaves it, breaking wind like a donkey, and does not re-enter it until morning.'" — Abu Muhammad said: "Ḍa'il means thin, shakhit means emaciated, ḍali' means well-ribbed, and khabaj means flatulence."
2637.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang laki-laki dari sahabat Muhammad bergulat dengan jin dan laki-laki itu mengalahkan jin tersebut. Laki-laki itu berkata kepada jin: 'Aku melihatmu kecil dan kurus — lenganmu seperti lengan anjing. Apakah begitu semua jin, ataukah hanya kamu?' Jin itu menjawab: 'Tidak, demi Allah, aku termasuk yang paling kekar di antara mereka. Tetapi bergulatlah denganku sekali lagi, dan jika kamu mengalahkanku, aku akan mengajarkan sesuatu yang bermanfaat bagimu.' Laki-laki itu bergulat lagi dan mengalahkannya. Ia berkata: 'Berikan — ajarkan kepadaku.' Jin itu berkata: 'Baik. Apakah kamu membaca Ayat Kursi: "Allah, tidak ada ilah selain Dia, Yang Maha Hidup lagi Maha Berdiri Sendiri" [Al-Baqarah: 255]?' Ia menjawab: 'Ya.' Jin itu berkata: 'Jika kamu membacanya di suatu rumah, setan akan keluar darinya sambil kentut seperti keledai, dan tidak akan masuk ke dalamnya lagi hingga pagi.'" — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Dha'il artinya kurus, shakhit artinya kempot, dhali' artinya berotot, dan khabaj artinya kentut."

2638.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites ten verses from Surah al-Baqarah in a night, no devil shall enter that house that night until morning — four from its beginning, the Verse of the Throne, the two verses after it, and three from its end, beginning with: 'To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Whether you show what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it' [Al-Baqarah: 284]."
2638.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca sepuluh ayat dari Surah al-Baqarah pada malam hari, tidak ada setan yang akan masuk ke rumah itu malam itu hingga pagi — empat dari awalnya, Ayat Kursi, dua ayat setelahnya, dan tiga dari akhirnya, dimulai dari: 'Milik Allah segala yang ada di langit dan di bumi. Apabila kamu menampakkan apa yang ada dalam dirimu atau menyembunyikannya, Allah akan membuat perhitungan denganmu atasnya' [Al-Baqarah: 284]."

2639.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites four verses from the beginning of Surah al-Baqarah, the Verse of the Throne, the two verses after the Verse of the Throne, and three from the end of Surah al-Baqarah — no devil shall come near him or his family that day, and nothing he dislikes shall befall him. And if these verses are recited over a person afflicted with madness, he will recover."
2639.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca empat ayat dari awal Surah al-Baqarah, Ayat Kursi, dua ayat setelah Ayat Kursi, dan tiga dari akhir Surah al-Baqarah — tidak ada setan yang akan mendekatinya maupun keluarganya hari itu, dan tidak ada sesuatu yang dibencinya yang akan menimpanya. Dan jika ayat-ayat ini dibacakan kepada orang yang gila, ia akan sembuh."

2640.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "I would not think that any person of sound mind would sleep before reciting the final verses of Surah al-Baqarah — for they are from a treasure beneath the Throne."
2640.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku tidak mengira ada orang yang berakal yang tidur sebelum membaca ayat-ayat terakhir Surah al-Baqarah — karena sesungguhnya ia berasal dari perbendaharaan di bawah 'Arsy."

2641.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Mughira ibn Subi' (may Allah be pleased with him) — who was a companion of 'Abdullah — that he said: "Whoever recites ten verses from al-Baqarah before sleeping will not forget the Quran: four verses from its beginning, the Verse of the Throne, the two verses after it, and three from its end." Ishaq said: "'Will not forget' means he will not forget what he has already memorized." Abu Muhammad added: "Some say: al-Mughira ibn Sami'."
2641.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Mughirah ibn Subi' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — yang merupakan salah seorang sahabat 'Abdullah — bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca sepuluh ayat dari al-Baqarah sebelum tidur, ia tidak akan melupakan Al-Qur'an: empat ayat dari awalnya, Ayat Kursi, dua ayat setelahnya, dan tiga dari akhirnya." Ishaq berkata: "'Tidak akan lupa' artinya tidak akan lupa apa yang telah ia hafal." Abu Muhammad menambahkan: "Sebagian menyebutnya: al-Mughirah ibn Sami'."

2642.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever recites the Verse of the Throne and the opening of Ha-Mim al-Mu'min up to His words 'the Forgiver of sin, the Acceptor of repentance, the severe in punishment, the Bestower of favour — there is no deity except Him, to Whom is the final destination' [Ghafir: 3] in the morning — no evil shall befall him until evening. And whoever recites them in the evening — no evil shall befall him until morning."
2642.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurayrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa membaca Ayat Kursi dan pembuka Ha-Mim al-Mu'min hingga firman-Nya 'Pengampun dosa, Penerima tobat, yang sangat keras hukuman-Nya, yang memiliki karunia — tidak ada ilah selain Dia, kepada-Nyalah tempat kembali' [Ghafir: 3] di pagi hari — tidak ada kejelekan yang akan menimpanya hingga sore. Dan barangsiapa membacanya di sore hari — tidak ada kejelekan yang akan menimpanya hingga pagi."

2643.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Nu'man ibn Bashir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah wrote a scripture two thousand years before He created the heavens and the earth, and from it He sent down two verses with which He concluded Surah al-Baqarah. If they are not recited in a dwelling for three nights, no devil shall come near it."
2643.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Nu'man ibn Bashir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah menulis sebuah kitab dua ribu tahun sebelum Ia menciptakan langit dan bumi, dan dari kitab itu Ia menurunkan dua ayat yang menutup Surah al-Baqarah. Jika keduanya tidak dibacakan di suatu tempat tinggal selama tiga malam, tidak ada setan yang akan mendekatinya."

2644.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Whoever recites the two final verses of Surah al-Baqarah in a night, they shall suffice him."
2644.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa membaca dua ayat terakhir Surah al-Baqarah pada suatu malam, keduanya akan mencukupinya."

2645.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma' bint Yazid (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Greatest Name of Allah is contained in these two verses: 'Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining' [Al-Baqarah: 255], and 'Your god is one God' [Al-Baqarah: 163]."
2645.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma' bint Yazid (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Nama Allah yang Paling Agung terdapat dalam dua ayat ini: 'Allah, tidak ada ilah selain Dia, Yang Maha Hidup lagi Maha Berdiri Sendiri' [Al-Baqarah: 255], dan 'Tuhan kalian adalah satu Tuhan' [Al-Baqarah: 163]."

2646.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jubayr ibn Nufayr that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah concluded Surah al-Baqarah with two verses that I was given from His treasury beneath the Throne — so learn them and teach them to your women, for they are a prayer, a recitation of the Quran, and a supplication."
2646.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jubayr ibn Nufayr bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah menutup Surah al-Baqarah dengan dua ayat yang diberikan kepadaku dari perbendaharaan-Nya di bawah 'Arsy — maka pelajarilah keduanya dan ajarkanlah kepada para wanita kalian, karena keduanya adalah shalat, bacaan Al-Qur'an, dan doa."

2647.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ and heard him say: 'Learn Surah al-Baqarah, for taking it is a blessing and abandoning it is a loss, and the sorcerers cannot withstand it.' He then fell silent for a moment, then said: 'Learn Surah al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran, for they are the two luminous ones, and they shall shade their companion on the Day of Judgment as if they were two clouds, or two canopies, or two flocks of birds in formation. The Quran shall meet its companion on the Day of Judgment when the grave opens for him — like a pale man — and say to him: "Do you recognize me?" He will say: "I do not recognize you." It will say: "I am your companion the Quran, who made you thirsty on hot days and kept you awake at night. Every merchant benefits from his trade, and today you benefit from every trade." He shall be given sovereignty in his right hand, eternity in his left, and a crown of dignity placed on his head. His parents shall be clothed in two garments whose value the entire world cannot match, and they shall say: "Why were we clothed in this?" And it will be said: "Because your child took up the Quran." Then he will be told: "Recite and ascend through the levels and chambers of Paradise" — and he shall keep ascending as long as he recites, whether he recites swiftly or slowly.'"
2647.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ dan mendengar beliau bersabda: 'Pelajarilah Surah al-Baqarah, karena mengambilnya adalah berkah dan meninggalkannya adalah kerugian, dan para tukang sihir tidak mampu menandinginya.' Kemudian beliau diam sejenak, lalu bersabda: 'Pelajarilah Surah al-Baqarah dan Ali 'Imran, karena keduanya adalah az-Zahrawain (dua yang bercahaya), dan keduanya akan menaungi pemiliknya pada hari kiamat seolah-olah keduanya adalah dua awan, atau dua naungan, atau dua kelompok burung yang berbaris rapat. Al-Qur'an akan menemui pemiliknya pada hari kiamat ketika kuburannya terbuka — seperti seorang pria yang pucat — dan berkata kepadanya: "Apakah kamu mengenaliku?" Ia akan berkata: "Aku tidak mengenalmu." Al-Qur'an berkata: "Akulah temanmu Al-Qur'an, yang dahulu membuatmu haus di siang yang terik dan membuat malammu terjaga. Setiap pedagang mendapat manfaat dari dagangannya, dan hari ini kamu mendapat manfaat dari setiap perdagangan." Ia diberi kerajaan di tangan kanannya, keabadian di tangan kirinya, dan mahkota kemuliaan diletakkan di kepalanya. Kedua orang tuanya akan dipakaikan dua pakaian yang nilainya tidak bisa ditaksir oleh seluruh dunia, lalu keduanya berkata: "Mengapa kami dipakaikan ini?" Dan dikatakan: "Karena anakmu mengambil Al-Qur'an." Kemudian kepadanya dikatakan: "Bacalah dan naiklah melalui tingkatan-tingkatan dan kamar-kamar surga" — dan ia terus naik selama ia membaca, baik ia membaca dengan cepat maupun perlahan-lahan.'"

2648.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "A brother of yours was shown a vision in which he saw people traversing a narrow crack in a high, rugged mountain. At the top of the mountain were two green trees calling out: 'Is there among you one who recites Surah al-Baqarah? Is there among you one who recites Surah Al 'Imran?' When a man said 'Yes,' the two trees drew near with their branches until he could grasp them and they carried him across the mountain." — Abu Muhammad said: "Al-a'dhaq means branches."
2648.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang saudara kalian diperlihatkan dalam mimpi bahwa orang-orang sedang melewati celah sempit di sebuah gunung yang tinggi dan terjal. Di puncak gunung itu ada dua pohon hijau yang memanggil-manggil: 'Adakah di antara kalian yang membaca Surah al-Baqarah? Adakah di antara kalian yang membaca Surah Ali 'Imran?' Ketika seorang laki-laki berkata 'Ya,' kedua pohon itu mendekat dengan ranting-rantingnya hingga ia bisa berpegangan padanya, lalu keduanya membawanya melintasi gunung." — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Al-a'dhaq artinya ranting-ranting."

2649.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "A man recited Surah al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran before 'Abdullah, and 'Abdullah said: 'You have recited two surahs that contain the Greatest Name of Allah — He who, when called upon by it, answers, and when asked by it, gives.'"
2649.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang laki-laki membaca Surah al-Baqarah dan Ali 'Imran di hadapan 'Abdullah, lalu 'Abdullah berkata: 'Kamu telah membaca dua surah yang di dalamnya terdapat Nama Allah yang Paling Agung — Dia yang jika dipanggil dengan nama itu, Ia menjawab, dan jika diminta dengan nama itu, Ia memberi.'"

2650.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran, the two shall come on the Day of Judgment saying: 'Our Lord, there is no path against him.'"
2650.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca al-Baqarah dan Ali 'Imran, keduanya akan datang pada hari kiamat sambil berkata: 'Wahai Tuhan kami, tidak ada jalan untuk menghukumnya.'"

2651.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites Al 'Imran is rich, and al-Nisa' is an adornment." — Abu Muhammad said: "Muḥabbira means beautifying."
2651.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Ali 'Imran, ia kaya. Dan al-Nisa' adalah perhiasan." — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Muḥabbira artinya memperindah."

2652.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Uthman ibn 'Affan (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Whoever recites the final portion of Al 'Imran in a night, he shall be recorded as having stood in prayer all night."
2652.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Utsman ibn 'Affan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca bagian akhir Ali 'Imran pada suatu malam, akan dicatat baginya pahala qiyamul lail sepanjang malam."

2653.iwh_en: It was narrated from Makhul that he said: "Whoever recites Surah Al 'Imran on Friday, the angels shall invoke blessings upon him until nightfall."
2653.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Makhul bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca Surah Ali 'Imran pada hari Jumat, para malaikat akan bershalawat untuknya hingga malam tiba."

2654.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) via al-Sha'bi that he said: "What an excellent treasure for the destitute is Surah Al 'Imran — reciting it in the final hours of the night."
2654.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui al-Sya'bi bahwa ia berkata: "Alangkah baiknya harta bagi orang yang tidak berharta yaitu Surah Ali 'Imran — membacanya di akhir malam."

2655.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Salil that he said: "A man had shed blood and took refuge in Wadi Majanna — a valley in which no one would spend the night without being bitten by a snake. Two monks were at the edge of the valley. One of them said to his companion: 'By Allah, the man is doomed.' Then the man began reciting Surah Al 'Imran. The two monks said: 'He is reciting a good surah — perhaps he will be saved.' And he woke up the next morning unharmed." — Abu Muhammad said: "Abu al-Salil is Durayb ibn Naqir, and some say ibn Nafir."
2655.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Salil bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang laki-laki yang telah menumpahkan darah berlindung di Wadi Majanna — sebuah lembah yang siapapun bermalam di dalamnya pasti digigit ular. Di tepi lembah itu ada dua rahib. Salah satu dari mereka berkata kepada temannya: 'Demi Allah, laki-laki itu pasti binasa.' Kemudian laki-laki itu mulai membaca Surah Ali 'Imran. Kedua rahib itu berkata: 'Ia membaca surah yang baik — semoga ia selamat.' Dan ia pun bangun pagi dalam keadaan selamat." — Abu Muhammad berkata: "Abu al-Salil adalah Durayb ibn Naqir, sebagian mengatakan ibn Nafir."

2656.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The Seven Long Surahs are like the Torah, the Mi'in are like the Gospel, the Mathani are like the Psalms, and the rest of the Quran is a distinction beyond them."
2656.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "As-Sab'ul Thuwal (tujuh surah panjang) seperti Taurat, al-Mi'in seperti Injil, al-Matsani seperti Zabur, dan selebihnya dari Al-Qur'an adalah keutamaan di atasnya."

2657.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "Surah al-An'am is among the most excellent surahs of the Quran."
2657.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Surah al-An'am termasuk surah-surah Al-Qur'an yang paling utama."

2658.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The opening of the Torah is Surah al-An'am, and its closing is Surah Hud."
2658.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Pembuka Taurat adalah Surah al-An'am, dan penutupnya adalah Surah Hud."

2659.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Recite Surah Hud on Fridays."
2659.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Bacalah Surah Hud pada hari Jumat."

2660.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Recite Surah Hud on Fridays."
2660.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Bacalah Surah Hud pada hari Jumat."

2661.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn Ma'dan that he said: "Whoever recites ten verses from Surah al-Kahf shall not fear the Dajjal."
2661.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn Ma'dan bahwa ia berkata: "Barangsiapa membaca sepuluh ayat dari Surah al-Kahf, ia tidak akan takut kepada Dajjal."
